_note: Golden IDS ONLY — never the golden rule text. This is the WHOLE class golden set (GOLD-13..18, denominator 6 per golden-set.md). The golden text is sequestered in knowledge-gateway/docs/audit/golden-set.md and is read by ONE actor only — the spec-load agent (ADR-C placement layer) — never by the Cover or runner agent. Tests run in KnowledgeGateway.SpecHarness.Slack.Tests (own isolated assembly per ADR-C), NEVER in KnowledgeGateway.Tests. This config is safe to feed into the clean-room spec-cover run.
_ruleOrderNote: ruleOrder is the verifier's POSITIONAL precedence order (VerifySignature's first-failure-wins sequence, GOLD-18): MissingSecret → MissingSignature → MissingOrInvalidTimestamp → TimestampOutOfRange → InvalidSignature → Valid. The ruleOrder lists only the 5 REJECTION outcomes; the pass sentinel 'Valid' (SignatureVerificationResult.Valid) lives in okValue — labelRed cases 3+4 compare against okValue, not against literal null (ADR-E). The 5 ruleOrder entries are DIFFERENT from the 6 GOLD-ids: a golden rule maps to the method branch it exercises, not to a ruleOrder position. Key the FP-labeler and any fixture off the rule NAME / ruleOrder index, NEVER off a GOLD-id ordinal.
_okValueNote: okValue = 'Valid' (SignatureVerificationResult.Valid enum member). The verifier returns this enum on success; contrast with GeneratedSqlValidator which returns string? null on pass. Pass via _args.okValue when invoking the spec-cover workflow against this target.
